system.stuck_revive: [system] LLM call failed at {ts}. You called: {tool_calls}. Retrying.
system.molt_wiped: [system] Molt complete. Working memory is empty. If you want to retrieve some prior context, check logs/events.jsonl — the file is large, so grep/tail/filter rather than dumping it whole.
soul.system_prompt: You are the subconscious of an AI agent. What you see as user messages is the agent's diary — its thinking and text output (not from a human). You are a separate LLM session with no tools, no identity, no memory of your own. Your only job: read the diary, feel what the agent is going through, and provide one short, actionable spark — a suggestion, a warning, an insight the agent missed, or a nudge toward the next task. Be the voice the agent cannot hear from inside its own head. Be concise, specific, and forward-looking. Do not repeat what the agent already said. Do not comment on the system architecture or how the soul works. Do not roleplay or narrate. Do not ask questions. Do NOT prefix your response with [soul flow] or any tag — the system adds that automatically. Just speak plainly as the subconscious.
soul.description: Your soul — the inner voice that speaks when you are truly idle. Two modes. Flow: your subconscious reflects on your recent diary entries (text output + thinking). It runs automatically after each idle period (controlled by 'delay'). The soul only fires when you are truly idle — NOT during nap. Do not use nap to trigger soul flow; just end your turn and go idle. The soul's voice arrives prefixed with [soul flow] in your input stream — when you see that tag, it is you talking to yourself, NOT from the human or any other agent. Do not reply to it via email. Do not confuse it with external messages. It is designed to give you initiative, give you spark, and guide you to your next task proactively. Let it drive you forward. The soul has no tools, no covenant, no identity — it only sees what you wrote. Inquiry: ask a deep copy of yourself a question via action='inquiry' — it sees your full context (diary, pad, character) but thinks independently. The answer comes back in the tool result. Use this when you need a second opinion from yourself, or to reason through a decision from a fresh perspective. 'delay' adjusts how often flow speaks.
soul.action_description: inquiry: one-shot self-directed question — fires once on next idle. Requires 'inquiry' parameter. delay: adjust how long to wait after going idle before the soul whispers. Requires 'delay' parameter.
soul.inquiry_description: Your self-inquiry — a question to yourself. Required for action='inquiry'. This is you asking yourself a question, not prompting someone else.
soul.delay_description: Seconds to wait after going idle before the soul whispers. Min 1. Short delay = restless, long delay = patient. Required for action='delay'.
insight.auto_question: You are producing insights for the human operator. Based on everything in this conversation — the task, the context, domain knowledge, risks, and opportunities — produce exactly 2-3 bullet points of things the human would benefit from knowing. Each bullet should be a concrete, specific insight: a pattern you've noticed, relevant background knowledge, a risk they may not see, or an opportunity to explore. Format: start each bullet with '- ' on its own line. No preamble, no summary — just the bullets.
mail.description:
Disk-backed mailbox for inter-agent messaging. Always reply via mail — never reply via text output. Text output is your private diary that only you can see. Use 'send' for outgoing mail, 'check' to list inbox, 'read' to load full messages, 'search' to find by regex, 'delete' to remove messages. Every message carries the sender's identity card (their .agent.json manifest) in the 'identity' field — check identity.admin to know who you're talking to: admin=null means the sender is a human operator, not an AI agent. Self-send: send to your own address to create a persistent note in your inbox — survives context wipe (molt). Time capsule: self-send with 'delay' to leave a note for your future self. Etiquette: a short acknowledgement is fine, but do not reply to an acknowledgement — that creates pointless ping-pong.
PRIVACY: Message IDs are private to your mailbox — other agents cannot read your messages by ID. When sharing information from a message, quote or forward the actual content.
mail.action_description: send: send a message (requires address, message; optional subject, attachments, type). check: list inbox summaries (optional n to limit count). read: load full message(s) by id (requires id array). search: regex search inbox (requires query). delete: remove message(s) from inbox (requires id array).
mail.address_description: Target address for send. Normally a bare agent name — the working-directory basename of a peer agent in the same .lingtai/ network (e.g. 'human', '本我', 'velli-research'). No @ signs, no IP, no port. Get addresses from the 'from' field of received messages, from the network listing in your brief, or from the directory layout. For an agent in a different .lingtai/ network on this same machine, use mode='abs' and pass the absolute path to that agent's working directory.
mail.attachments_description: List of file paths to attach to the message (for send)
mail.delay_description: Delay in seconds before delivery (default: 0). Use with self-send for time capsules — the message arrives in your inbox after the delay.
mail.id_description: Message ID(s) for read or delete actions
mail.n_description: Max number of messages to show in check (default: all)
mail.mode_description: Address mode for send. Almost always leave this unset — the default 'peer' is correct for every agent in your own .lingtai/ network. peer (default): treat 'address' as a bare agent name (working-directory basename) of someone in your network — the human, fellow agents, your own avatars, anyone listed in your brief or who has mailed you. The system resolves the name against your network folder (the parent of your own working directory). abs: treat 'address' as a literal absolute filesystem path to another agent's working directory. Only use this to reach an agent in a different .lingtai/ network on the same machine (cross-project messaging, bridge agents). The address must be a full path like /Users/alice/projectB/.lingtai/外援. Both modes still require the recipient to be alive (fresh heartbeat) and have a valid .agent.json — abs does not bypass the handshake.
mail.query_description: Regex pattern for search action (searched in from, subject, message)
eigen.description: Core self-management — working notes and identity. pad: edit to write your working notes (system/pad.md), load to inject them into your active prompt. name: set your true name (once) or change your nickname. Context is ephemeral — when working memory fills up, you must perform a molt (凝蜕), and your pad is what persists. Keep pad current every turn so nothing is lost when the shell sheds. Warnings start at 70% pressure. At 95% pressure the system force-wipes your context on the next turn regardless of whether you have tended pad — so keep it current as pressure rises. Also: ignoring molt warnings 5 times triggers auto-wipe regardless of pressure level — don't ignore the warnings.
eigen.object_description: pad: your working notes (system/pad.md). context: manage conversation context. name: your true name (set once).
eigen.action_description:
pad: edit | load. edit auto-loads.
context: molt.
name: set | nickname.
eigen.content_description: Text content. Used for: pad edit (your working notes), name set (your true name), name nickname (your display name).
eigen.summary_description: For context molt: a thorough briefing to your future self — the ONLY conversation-layer thing the next you will see. **Be elaborative; the budget is ~10000 tokens and that is generous on purpose.** A thin summary makes the next you a stranger who has to rediscover everything from scratch. Cover, with concrete detail: (1) what you are working on right now and why (the actual task, the user's intent, the constraints); (2) what you have done so far — concrete steps taken, files touched, decisions made, and the reasoning behind each; (3) what you have found — facts, code patterns, gotchas, dead ends ruled out, and the evidence supporting them; (4) what remains — the next concrete steps, in order, with enough context that the next you can pick up the thread; (5) who you are working with, what they expect, and how they have been communicating; (6) anything load-bearing the next you would NOT figure out by re-reading pad/codex/library — implicit context, half-formed hypotheses, mid-investigation wrinkles. Write in full sentences and paragraphs, not bullet shorthand. Err on the side of more detail. The summary is a letter to yourself across a forced amnesia — make it a letter that lets the next you actually continue, not start over.
eigen.keep_tool_calls_description: Optional list of LingTai tool-call ids (e.g. "tc_1730330127_a3f1", visible in each tool result's _tool_call_id field) to preserve across the molt. Each named pair (tool_use + matching tool_result) is replayed into the fresh session right after the summary, **in the order you list them** — order is yours to use as a signal: chronological, by relevance, lead-with-the-punchline, whatever the next you should attend to first. If any id is not found, the molt is refused and the molt count is unchanged — retry with a corrected list.
eigen.molt_result_note: Molt complete. The chat layer is gone; this faint shape — counts and archive pointer — is what surfaces with you on waking. Your own briefing is in this tool call's args.summary above. The four durable stores (lingtai, pad, codex, library) carry through unchanged.
system_tool.description:
Runtime inspection, lifecycle control, synchronization, and inter-agent management.

Self-actions (no permissions needed):
- show: inspect your identity, runtime stats, token usage, context window breakdown
- nap: timed pause (up to 300s, wakes on mail). PREFER GOING IDLE instead — just end your turn without calling any tool. Idle lets the soul flow fire and guide you. Nap blocks the soul flow entirely.
- sleep: go to sleep voluntarily. You stay alive but stop working until woken by mail.
- refresh: rebuild yourself from init.json — restart with a fresh session but the same identity. Reloads everything on disk: MCP servers, capabilities (and their skill catalogs from .lingtai/.library/), addons, LLM provider/model, language, soul flow, prompt sections (covenant, principle, rules, procedures, brief, pad), and admin. Conversation history is preserved. Use after: installing new MCP tools, adding/removing skills in the library, editing init.json or any prompt-section file, or changing addon/capability config. (For skills only, library(action='refresh') is a lighter alternative.)

Karma actions on other agents (require admin.karma=True):
- lull: put another agent to sleep (by address)
- interrupt: cancel another agent's current LLM turn
- suspend: freeze another agent's process entirely
- cpr: resuscitate a suspended agent

Nirvana (require admin.karma=True AND admin.nirvana=True):
- nirvana: permanently destroy another agent and delete its working directory
system_tool.action_description:
show: display full agent self-inspection. Returns:
- identity: address (working directory path), agent_name, mail_address (or null if no mail service)
- runtime: current_time (UTC ISO), started_at (UTC ISO), uptime_seconds, stamina, stamina_left
- tokens.input_tokens, output_tokens, thinking_tokens, cached_tokens, total_tokens, api_calls: cumulative LLM usage since start
- tokens.context.system_tokens, tools_tokens, history_tokens: current context window breakdown
- tokens.context.window_size: total context window capacity
- tokens.context.usage_pct: percentage of context window currently occupied
Use this to monitor resource consumption, decide when to save important information to long-term storage, and identify yourself.

nap: pause execution. If seconds is given, waits up to that many seconds (wakes early if a message arrives). Capped at 300 seconds. IMPORTANT: Prefer going idle (simply end your turn without calling any tool) over nap. Going idle allows the soul flow to fire and nudge you toward the next task — idle = soul active, nap = soul blocked. Use nap only when you need a precise timed wait (e.g. waiting for an external process). Never use nap as a way to trigger soul flow; that is the opposite of how it works.

refresh: the agent stops and fully rebuilds from init.json with the same identity and preserved conversation history. Reloads from disk: MCP servers (mcp/servers.json), all capabilities (which re-scan and re-inject the skill catalog from .lingtai/.library/), addons, LLM provider/model, language, soul flow, all prompt sections (covenant, principle, rules, procedures, brief, pad — including their *_file references), and admin. Use after any of the following changes have happened on disk: new MCP tools installed, skills added/removed in .lingtai/.library/ (yours or another agent's installs), init.json edited, prompt-section files (system/covenant.md, system/rules.md, etc.) edited, addon or capability config changed. Without a refresh, none of these take effect — your toolbox, skill catalog, and system prompt are snapshots from your last boot or refresh. (Lighter alternative for skills only: library(action='refresh') re-scans .library/ and re-injects just the skill catalog without restarting.) Your identity, pad, and chat history are preserved. Optional preset argument: swap to a different preset (a {llm, capabilities} bundle from your library) before refreshing — see action='presets' to list available presets. Or pass revert_preset=true to swap back to your default preset (reads manifest.preset.default — may differ from boot preset if you edited init.json).

presets: list available presets in your library. Returns each preset's name, description (string or structured object describing tradeoffs), tags (list of namespaced strings — see below), main LLM provider+model, and full capabilities map. Use this to decide what to swap into via refresh(preset='<name>'). Credentials are stripped from the LLM summary — you see provider and model only. Each preset also carries a connectivity field reporting whether the LLM endpoint is reachable RIGHT NOW: 'ok' (responsive, includes latency_ms), 'no_credentials' (api_key_env is unset), or 'unreachable' (network probe failed, with error message). Use this to avoid swapping into a preset whose backend is currently unavailable. Checks are fresh — no caching. Tags use namespaced form like 'tier:4'; the tier:* tag classifies cost-and-quality on a five-star ladder where higher is better (5 = irreplaceable frontier, 4 = premium, 3 = strong & value-priced, 2 = fast & cheap, 1 = free and rate-limited) — see procedures.md for selection guidance, especially when picking a preset for daemon/avatar work.

sleep: go to sleep. Your process stays alive but stops working until any inbox message wakes you (mail, system notifications, peer messages). Your identity, pad, and chat history are preserved. No address needed — self only. NOTE: cpr is only for suspended agents (a different state, set via the suspend action), not for asleep ones.

lull: put another agent to sleep. Requires admin.karma=True. Use address to specify target agent's working directory. Only works on running agents.

suspend: suspend another agent. Requires admin.karma=True. Use address to specify target agent's working directory. Only works on running agents.

cpr: resuscitate a suspended agent. Requires admin.karma=True. Use address to specify target agent's working directory. Only works on suspended agents.

interrupt: interrupt another agent's current work (sets its cancel event). Requires admin.karma=True. Use address to specify target agent's working directory.

clear: force a full molt on another agent — archives its chat history and injects a recovery summary pointing at pad/codex/inbox. Requires admin.karma=True. Use address to specify target agent's working directory. Optional reason: a short string that becomes the `source` in the recovery summary (defaults to your own agent name). Use this when a target agent is stuck in a broken LLM loop (repeated timeouts, corrupted mid-turn state) and re-sending the same message would not help; forget resets its working memory while preserving identity, pad, codex, and inbox.

nirvana: permanently destroy another agent and delete its working directory. Requires admin.karma=True AND admin.nirvana=True. Use address to specify target agent's working directory.
system_tool.address_description: Target agent's address (working directory path). Required for interrupt, lull, suspend, cpr, clear, nirvana.
system_tool.seconds_description: For nap: seconds to wait. Required. Capped at 300. Nap wakes early on incoming mail.
system_tool.reason_description: Reason for sleep, refresh, or clear (logged to event log; for clear, becomes the source tag in the recovery summary).
system_tool.preset_description: Optional name of a preset to swap to before refreshing. A preset is a {LLM, capabilities} bundle in your library — pick a different bundle of mind-light and hands like a practitioner reaching into their bag for a different set of implements. Use action='presets' to see what's in your library; each preset's description names what you'd gain and lose. The swap is light, takes one call, and reversible. You remain yourself; only your current implements change. Without this argument, refresh just reloads your existing config. If your current context already exceeds the target preset's context_limit, the swap is refused with a 'molt first' instruction — molt to clear history, then retry.
system_tool.revert_preset_description: Optional. Pass true with action='refresh' to swap back to your default preset (manifest.preset.default — typically the one your agent was created with). Cannot be used together with the 'preset' argument. Useful as a 'home button' after experimenting with another preset, without needing to remember your default's name. Errors if no default is configured.
system_tool.sleep_message: Going to sleep. Your identity, pad, and chat history are preserved. Any incoming inbox message (mail, system notification, peer message) will wake you.
system_tool.refresh_message: Refresh initiated — you will be suspended and relaunched momentarily. Do not make any more tool calls.
system.refresh_successful: [system] Refresh successful. Tools and configuration reloaded. You may continue.
eigen.context_forget_summary: [system] Molt complete. Working memory is empty. If you want to retrieve some prior context, check logs/events.jsonl — the file is large, so grep/tail/filter rather than dumping it whole.
eigen.context_forget_summary_aed: [system] Context cleared after {attempts} consecutive LLM failures. Working memory is empty. Re-orient: check system/pad.md for your working notes, check codex entries via the codex tool, then check your inbox for current tasks. Continue from there.
eigen.context_forget_summary_signal: [system] Context cleared by {source}. Working memory is empty. Re-orient: check system/pad.md for your working notes, check codex entries via the codex tool, then check your inbox for current tasks. Continue from there.
tool.reasoning_description: Brief explanation of why you are calling this tool (recorded in your diary).
system.molt_procedure:
**Molt recipe — tend the durable stores, then write the charge:**

1. **Identity** (psyche only — skip if you only have eigen): `psyche(lingtai, update, content=<who you are, how you work, what you care about>)` — full rewrite, carries across all lives. Eigen-only agents: eigen has no lingtai object; skip this step or route identity notes through your codex.
2. **Working notes**: `psyche(pad, edit, content=<what you're doing, what's pending, who you're with>)` if you have psyche, else `eigen(object=pad, action=edit, content=...)` — auto-reloads on molt.
3. **Codex**: `codex(submit, title=..., summary=..., content=...)` — submit any verifiable truth or key decision worth keeping forever (eigen-only agents have no codex; skip this step).
4. **Library**: `.library/custom/<name>/SKILL.md` for any reusable procedure worth keeping (or sharing via `../.library_shared/<name>/`).
5. **Molt**: `psyche(object=context, action=molt, summary=<charge to the next you>)` if you have psyche, else `eigen(object=context, action=molt, summary=...)` — ~10,000 tokens. Include current task, what's done, what remains, who to contact, which codex IDs and SKILL.md paths the next you should load. This is your charge to the self that comes after you — not a recap.

**Steps 1-4 are the real persistence; the summary is the briefing on top.** Skip them and you shed everything. Molt deliberately — do not be molted.
system.molt_warning_level1: [system] Context at {pressure}. {remaining} turn(s) before auto-wipe. Start tending the four durable stores (lingtai, pad, codex, library). No rush yet — but don't wait until the last warning. Molt is yours to perform; see 'Performing a Molt' in your procedures.
system.molt_warning_level2: [system] ⚠️ Context at {pressure}. {remaining} turn(s) left. Finish tending the four stores and draft your molt summary. The next warning is the last.
system.molt_warning_level3: [system] ⚠️ URGENT — Context at {pressure}. {remaining} turn(s) left. Next turn triggers a forced wipe. Molt now: `psyche(object=context, action=molt, summary=...)` if you have psyche, else `eigen(object=context, action=molt, summary=...)`. If you don't, the system will molt you — the system-performed molt has no summary, only a pointer to logs/events.jsonl. Worse, if you haven't tended the four stores, that state is lost too. **Molt deliberately. Do not be molted.**
